observations.content: Objetivo: mejorar UX de login con icono de ojo y añadir recuperación completa de contraseña. Hallazgos: no había flujo de recover/reset ni callback auth en App Router; se respetó frontera UI->services->infra. Cambios: nuevo contrato IAuthRepository para requestPasswordRecovery/updatePassword, casos de uso nuevos, endpoints /api/auth/recover y /api/auth/update-password, callback /auth/callback, páginas /recover-password y /reset-password, forms y tests UI/API/use-case, y enlace '¿Olvidaste la contraseña?' en login. Validación: lint y build en verde; test global con 1 fallo preexistente en GetAvailableSectionsUseCase.test.ts. Archivos clave: src/components/auth/*, src/services/auth/*, src/app/api/auth/{recover,update-password}/*, src/app/auth/callback/*, src/core/use-cases/auth/*, src/infrastructure/persistence/supabase/SupabaseAuthRepository.ts.

observations.title: Bugfix: hub tests - multiplayer policy alignment
observations.content: Fallaba CI en GetAvailableSectionsUseCase.test esperando MULTIPLAYER disponible con medallas. La política actual en HubAccessPolicy bloquea MULTIPLAYER siempre (feature en construcción), por lo que el test estaba desactualizado. Se actualizó la expectativa a false y el test vuelve a verde. Riesgo: si se habilita multiplayer en el futuro, habrá que ajustar HubAccessPolicy y este test juntos.

observations.title: Decision: Story mobile UX controls + pinch zoom
observations.content: Contexto: en Story mobile faltaba zoom por gesto y la barra inferior tenía acciones duplicadas/confusas. Acción: se implementó pinch-zoom real con dos dedos en StoryCircuitMap usando applyPinchZoom y recentrado de cámara; se movió Exit fuera del panel de detalle y ahora va en controles superiores junto a mute/centrar; se eliminó botón duplicado Táctica de la barra inferior; el panel de detalle móvil ahora entra desde la derecha y respeta espacio de la barra inferior para no taparla. Resultado: UX móvil más clara sin alterar layout desktop ni flujo de dominio Story. Riesgo pendiente: validar en dispositivos físicos distintos el gesto de pinch junto al drag de cámara.

observations.title: Discovery: audit-2026-04-16 security-next16-and-architecture-drift
observations.content: topic_key=discovery/audit-2026-04-16. Contexto: auditoría integral de calidad, estructura, mantenibilidad y seguridad. Hallazgos: pnpm lint/test/build/test:coverage en verde, pero pnpm audit --prod reporta vulnerabilidad HIGH en next 16.1.7 (GHSA-q4gf-8mx6-v5v3, parche >=16.2.3). Deuda estructural: 12 archivos productivos >150 líneas, especialmente StoryScene.tsx (316), StoryCircuitMap.tsx (204), create-story-scene-actions.ts (217), use-story-circuit-motion.ts (213), SupabaseOpponentRepository.ts (271), SupabaseAdminStoryDeckRepository.ts (211). Se observan imports directos de core/use-cases en componentes y lógica de dominio orquestada desde UI (p.ej. StoryDuelClient crea HeuristicOpponentStrategy y resuelve coin toss), indicando deriva de límites UI->services->core. Cobertura global 86.89% statements, pero hay use-cases críticos a 0%: DeleteAdminMarketPackUseCase.ts, UpsertAdminMarketListingUseCase.ts, RemoveCardFromFusionDeckUseCase.ts. Documentación principal no incluye sección explícita de funcionalidades, URL pública de despliegue ni enlace de slides.

observations.title: Session Summary: auditoria integral 2026-04-16
observations.content: topic_key=summary/audit-2026-04-16. Objetivo: evaluar estado de calidad, seguridad, arquitectura, mantenibilidad y documentación de entrega. Validaciones ejecutadas: pnpm lint OK, pnpm test OK (290 files, 723 tests), pnpm build OK, pnpm test:coverage OK (86.89/75.82/92.28/90.7), pnpm security:rate-limit:check* OK con warning en generic por timeout default. Hallazgo crítico: pnpm audit --prod falla por vulnerabilidad high en next 16.1.7 (parche >=16.2.3). Hallazgos de estructura: 12 archivos productivos superan 150 líneas; 6 archivos incumplen formato estricto de cabecera de ruta+descripción. Hallazgos de arquitectura: múltiples componentes/app cliente importan core/use-cases y ejecutan orquestación de dominio en capa UI (ejemplos en StoryDuelClient y hooks de Board), desviando SoC. Hallazgos de cobertura: use-cases sin cobertura (DeleteAdminMarketPackUseCase, UpsertAdminMarketListingUseCase, RemoveCardFromFusionDeckUseCase). Hallazgos docs: README sin sección explícita de funcionalidades principales, sin URL pública de despliegue real y sin enlace de slides. Archivos evidencia clave: package.json, README.md, StoryScene.tsx, StoryDuelClient.tsx, StoryCircuitMap.tsx, use-story-circuit-motion.ts, create-story-scene-actions.ts, SupabaseOpponentRepository.ts, SupabaseAdminStoryDeckRepository.ts.

observations.title: Project audit: what's missing for production

**What**: Full audit of what's missing for professional production readiness.
**Why**: User asked what's needed to make the project fully professional and scalable.
**Where**: Project-wide
**Learned**:
- CRITICAL: No error.tsx or global-error.tsx anywhere — runtime crashes = white screen
- CRITICAL: No loading.tsx — no Suspense skeletons, poor UX
- CRITICAL: Leaked Password Protection disabled in Supabase Auth (WARN from advisor)

- IMPORTANT: No security headers (CSP, HSTS) configured

- IMPORTANT: No coverage threshold enforced in vitest

observations.title: Pre-commit hooks amb husky i lint-staged
observations.content:
**What**: Set up pre-commit hooks with husky + lint-staged.
**Why**: Nothing prevented bad code from being committed — lint and line checks only ran in CI, not locally. Found during production audit.
**Where**: .husky/pre-commit (npx lint-staged), package.json (lint-staged config)
**Learned**: 
- lint-staged runs ONLY on staged .ts/.tsx files
- husky init creates default hooks in _/ directory — must clean them up (only keep pre-commit)
- lint-staged config: eslint --fix + check-file-length on .ts/.tsx before commit
- Pre-commit will fail if eslint or check-file-length fails (commit blocked)

observations.title: Bugfix: husky usant pnpm exec en comptes de npx
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed husky pre-commit hook to use `pnpm exec lint-staged` instead of `npx lint-staged`.
**Why**: `npx` uses npm's cache and can execute malicious packages with the same name as legitimate tools (e.g., a malicious `eslint` package on npm could be installed instead of the intended one). This is a known supply chain attack vector.
**Where**: .husky/pre-commit
**Learned**: 
- husky hooks run outside pnpm context, so `npx` would bypass pnpm's lockfile guarantees
- Always use `pnpm exec <cmd>` or `pnpm <script>` for package scripts in hooks
- Added rule to AGENTS.md: "Prohibit npm executing scripts directly — use pnpm exec or package.json scripts"
- `pnpm exec lint-staged` works because lint-staged is in devDependencies and pnpm links bins correctly

observations.title: Rate limiting i security headers implementats
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented rate limiting (60 req/min per IP) and security headers in proxy.ts middleware.
**Why**: No protection against API abuse (contact, audit endpoints) and no security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame, etc.). Found during production audit.
**Where**: src/lib/rate-limit.ts (in-memory store with cleanup interval), src/proxy.ts (extended with rate limit check + security headers before i18n middleware)
**Learned**: 
- Rate limiting runs BEFORE intlMiddleware so abusive IPs are blocked early
- HSTS only added when x-forwarded-proto is http (redirect to https first)
- For multi-instance prod, replace in-memory Map with Redis (Upstash)
- Duplicate import of LearningXpEventRecord in learning-mappers.ts caused TS error — fixed by removing the duplicate line
- Branch feat/rate-limiting-security-headers created from feature/engram (which was itself from develop)

observations.title: Playwright E2E i coverage thresholds afegits
observations.content:
**What**: Added Playwright E2E testing and vitest coverage thresholds.
**Why**: E2E testing was in PRD but never implemented. Coverage had no minimum threshold. Both found during production audit.
**Where**: playwright.config.ts, tests/e2e/landing.spec.ts (4 tests), package.json (test:e2e scripts), vitest.config.ts (coverage thresholds: 50% lines/functions/branches/statements)
**Learned**:
- Vitest and Playwright both use `test.describe()` and `test()` — same file can't use both. Must exclude 'tests/e2e/**' and 'playwright.config.ts' from vitest include/exclude.
- Playwright E2E tests need a running dev server — webServer config handles this in playwright.config.ts
- Threshold started at 50% to avoid CI failures; can raise incrementally
- `pnpm test -- --run` only runs vitest (e2e excluded); E2E runs separately via `pnpm test:e2e`
- Branch feat/e2e-testing created from develop

observations.title: Bugfix: Vitest estava escanejant node_modules
observations.content:
**What**: Vitest was scanning entire repo including node_modules, causing CI failures from third-party test files (tsconfig-paths, style-to-js).
**Why**: vitest include had `**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}` which matched node_modules/.pnpm/**/*.test.ts files. 171 failures in CI from dependencies, not project code.
**Where**: vitest.config.ts (include narrowed to 'src/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}', 'tests/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}')
**Learned**: Always restrict test globs to src/ and tests/ directories, never use '**' that includes node_modules. Even with exclude, the include pattern was pulling in test files from pnpm symlinked node_modules.

observations.title: TypeScript error: images assets sense type declarations
observations.content:
**What**: Added `src/types/assets.d.ts` to declare image modules for TypeScript.
**Why**: TypeScript was failing `tsc --noEmit` on CI because image files imported via `@/assets/images/*.jpg` have no type declarations. These images exist locally but TypeScript in CI couldn't resolve them.
**Where**: src/types/assets.d.ts (glob declarations for jpg/png/webp/gif/svg)
**Learned**: The `StaticImageData` import in src/lib/data.ts was actually not used — the images were imported as string URLs (not StaticImageData). The fix is a simple module declaration, not changing how images are imported.

observations.title: Comprehensive project audit results
observations.content:
**What**: Performed a comprehensive audit of the digitai-studios Next.js project including lint, type-check, tests, architecture check, build verification, and common issue scans.
**Why**: User requested a full health check of the codebase to identify blockers and warnings.
**Where**: C:\Users\Boby\Desktop\Pagines web\NextJS\digitai-studios
**Learned**: 
- All 5 main checks pass (lint, tsc, tests, check, build).
- `rg` (ripgrep) is not installed on Windows, causing `check-architecture.mjs` to silently pass without actually scanning files (error.status === 1 returns empty string).
- Multiple `console.log` / `console.error` statements remain in production source files under `src/`.
- No actual Supabase `.from()` calls found in `.tsx` files under `src/`.
- No files exceed 150 lines outside the allowlist.
- Error boundaries and loading states exist at `[locale]`, `dashboard`, and `admin` levels.
- Husky pre-commit hook is active and runs `pnpm exec lint-staged`.
- `.env.example` is comprehensive and well-documented.
- Git working tree is clean with no uncommitted changes.

observations.title: Auditoria completa: proxy fix + architecture check cross-platform
observations.content:
**What**: Full audit revealed proxy export name bug and architecture check silently passing on Windows.
**Why**: Next.js 16 renamed middleware to proxy; architecture check used ripgrep which doesn't exist on Windows.
**Where**: src/proxy.ts (renamed export), scripts/check-architecture.mjs (replaced rg with globSync)
**Learned**: 
- Next.js 16 breaking change: middleware export renamed to proxy in proxy.ts
- Architecture check was a false positive on Windows — rg not found but script caught error.status===1 and returned empty string
- Replaced with Node.js built-in globSync for cross-platform compatibility
- All quality gates now pass: lint, tsc, 69 tests, check (lines + architecture), build

observations.title: Arquitectura d'agents per contingut educatiu
observations.content:
**What**: Created comprehensive architecture for AI agents to generate learning content (docs/learning-agents/)
**Why**: Need a scalable, modular system so future agents can generate quality educational content consistently with minimal token consumption
**Where**: docs/learning-agents/ (7 documents), branch feat/architecture-learning-content
**Learned**:
- Separated concerns: README (workflow), CONTENT-SCHEMA (data), TEMPLATES (copy-paste JSON), PEDAGOGY (quality rules), DATABASE-REFERENCE (SQL/Supabase), COURSE-ROADMAP (plan), INDEX (navigation)
- 13 step types defined with complete JSON schemas
- 6 tracks, 25 modules, 72 estimated lessons in roadmap
- Quality checklist prevents generic quiz-style content
- All content must be 'draft' until human review
- Pedagogical structure: 7-step lesson format (intro → concept → example → exercise → validation → summary → next step)
- Removed emoji from COURSE-ROADMAP to avoid JSON encoding issues in future automation

observations.title: Fix recorrent 404 després de canvis a proxy.ts
observations.content:
**What**: Implementades 3 mesures preventives per evitar que el bug de 404 en rutes dinàmiques i18n torni a passar després de modificar `src/proxy.ts`.
**Why**: Next.js 16 no invalida sempre la caché de resolució de rutes quan canvia el middleware. Les rutes estàtiques funcionen, però les dinàmiques (`[slug]`) fallen amb 404 fins que no esborres `.next`.
**Where**:
- `package.json`: nou script `"clean": "node -e \"require('fs').rmSync('.next', {recursive:true, force:true})\""` (cross-platform)
- `AGENTS.md`: Troubleshooting ampliat amb la causa arrel i la solució pas a pas (`pnpm clean && pnpm dev`)
- `src/proxy.ts`: capçalera amb advertència en català explicant el problema
**Learned**: No cal afegir `rimraf` com a dependència; `node -e "fs.rmSync(...)"` és cross-platform i immediat.

observations.title: Migració: parent_module_id per jerarquia de submòduls
observations.content:
**What**: Afegida columna `parent_module_id` a `learning_modules` per suportar submòduls (e.g., Windows → PowerShell). Inclou FK, constraint anti-cicles, i dos índexs.
**Why**: L'usuari vol un mapa visual de camí amb nodes que contenen sub-temes. Necessitem jerarquia a la BD.
**Where**: Migration `add_parent_module_id_to_learning_modules` aplicada a Supabase.
**Learned**: `ON DELETE SET NULL` és més segur que CASCADE per a contingut educatiu. Constraint `no_self_parent` evita cicles.

observations.title: Estructura jeràrquica de submòduls implementada
observations.content:
**What**: Implementada jerarquia de submòduls a learning_modules amb parent_module_id, service d'arbre (`learning-module-tree-service.ts`), action `getTrackModuleTree`, i component UI `LearningModulePath` estil Duolingo.
**Why**: L'usuari vol un mapa visual de camí amb nodes que contenen sub-temes (Windows→PowerShell, Linux→Ubuntu→Nginx).
**Where**:
- Migration `add_parent_module_id_to_learning_modules`
- Migration `insert_sistemes_operatius_track_and_modules`
- `src/services/learning/learning-module-tree-service.ts`
- `src/features/learning/ui/LearningModulePath.tsx`
- `src/actions/track-module-tree.ts`
- `src/app/[locale]/dashboard/learn/[moduleSlug]/page.tsx`
**Learned**: `module` és reserved word a Next.js (eslint `@next/next/no-assign-module-variable`). Usar `mod` en comptes.

observations.title: Estructura jeràrquica completada: 3 branques amb desbloqueig seqüencial
observations.content:
**What**: Reestructurat completament el track 'sistemes-informatics' amb 3 branques principals: Sistemes Operatius, Xarxes, Hardware. Cada branca té submòduls jeràrquics segons Contingut_formacio.mmd. Implementada lògica de desbloqueig: lliure entre branques (lateral), seqüencial dins cada branca.
**Why**: L'usuari vol que els alumnes puguin explorar qualsevol branca però progressin seqüencialment dins cada una. També s'han eliminat tracks duplicats.
**Where**:
- Migration `restructure_sistemes_informatics_with_branches`
- Migration `insert_ubuntu_submodules_and_network_hardware`
- `src/services/learning/learning-module-tree-service.ts` (sequential unlock logic)
- `src/features/learning/ui/LearningModulePath.tsx` (branch separators)
**Learned**: En dev mode tot està `active` per proves; en production es `locked` per defecte i `applySequentialUnlock` gestiona el desbloqueig. La funció `resolveModuleStatus` usa `NODE_ENV` per determinar el comportament.

observations.title: UI: Mapa de branques paral·leles per a mòduls jeràrquics
observations.content:
**What**: Redissenyat LearningModulePath per mostrar les 3 branques (SO, Xarxes, Hardware) en 3 columnes paral·leles en comptes d'una llista plana. Cada columna té el seu propi camí vertical amb connectors visuals.
**Why**: L'usuari volia veure la bifurcació visualment, no tot en fila. Ara es veu clarament que són 3 branques independents però dins el mateix track.
**Where**: `src/features/learning/ui/LearningModulePath.tsx`
**Learned**: No fer flatten de tot l'arbre quan es vol mostrar jerarquia visual. Les 3 columnes amb grid de Tailwind és la solució més neta per a responsive.

observations.title: Bugfix: Filtrar mòduls per track al detall de track
observations.content:
**What**: Corregit bug on `track-module-tree.ts` passava TOTS els mòduls de TOTS els tracks a `buildModuleTree`, resultant en barreja de contingut (ex: veure "Fonaments de programació" dins Sistemes Informàtics). Ara filtra `snapshot.modules` per `trackId === track.id` abans de construir l'arbre.
**Why**: `getDashboardSnapshot` retorna tots els mòduls de la BD sense filtrar per track. L'action de detall de track ha de filtrar només els del track actual.
**Where**: `src/actions/track-module-tree.ts`
**Learned**: Revisar sempre que les dades filtrades a nivell de repositori/action siguin les correctes abans de passar-les al component. El snapshot del dashboard global ha de servir tots els tracks, però el detall d'un track ha de restringir.

observations.title: Fix: Nodes no clicableables per rutes incorrectes d'href
observations.content:
**What**: Corregit bug on els href dels nodes usaven trackSlug (sistemes-informatics) en lloc del propi slug del mòdul (windows-10). Això feia que les rutes fossin incorrectes i els nodes no es poguessin clicar.

observations.content:
**What**: Creada nova action getLearningLessonByModule que busca el track a partir del moduleSlug, i modificat el page de la lliçó per usar-la. Això arregla el 404 quan es clica un node de lliçó.
**Why**: El page de la lliçó passava moduleSlug (ex: windows-10) a getLearningLessonRunner que esperava un trackSlug (ex: sistemes-informatics). La lliçó no es trobava perquè buscava un track inexistent.
**Where**:

observations.content:

**Why**: Els steps de contingut no requereixen interacció de l'usuari, només lectura. El botó ha de ser 'Següent' i sempre actiu.
**Where**: 
- src/features/learning/ui/lesson-runner/useLessonRunnerState.ts (hasAnswer)
- src/features/learning/ui/LearningLessonRunner.tsx (buttonLabel)
**Learned**: Sempre afegir casos especials per tipus de steps que no requereixen resposta quan es creen nous tipus (com 'content').

observations.title: Sempre posar publication_status='published' a lliçons i steps finals
observations.content:
**What**: Les lliçons noves no eren accessibles perquè es creaven amb publication_status='draft'. El filtre publishedContent requereix publication_status='published'.
**Why**: La norma diu que el contingut generat per agents ha de ser 'draft' fins a validació humana, però si l'usuari ja ha validat i vol provar-ho, cal canviar a 'published'.
**Where**: learning_lessons.publication_status i learning_steps.publication_status
**Learned**: Després de crear contingut amb Supabase MCP, SEMPRE verificar que publication_status sigui 'published' a les taules learning_lessons I learning_steps abans de dir-li a l'usuari que ho provi. També verificar que active=true.

observations.title: Bugfix: Auth session not persisting after registration/login
observations.content: Critical bug found and fixed: signIn from Server Action cannot set browser session cookie, causing immediate redirect back to login when navigating to protected routes (e.g., SAT). Fix: remove signIn from registerAction, redirect to /login?registered=true after registration. Fix loginAction: remove isRedirectError catch that was breaking Next-Auth signIn redirect flow. Add Suspense boundary for useSearchParams in login page. Commit: f6cb34d.

observations.title: Auth.js v5 Next.js 16 Proxy Pattern - Root Cause Analysis
observations.content:
CRITICAL: The proxy.ts in Next.js 16 CANNOT use 'await auth()' without request context. This was the root cause of the login failure chain:

1. loginAction called signIn() server-side → Next-Auth created session cookie in browser
2. Browser redirected to /dashboard with the cookie
3. proxy.ts ran 'await auth()' WITHOUT passing the HTTP request → auth() could NOT read browser cookies → returned null session
4. proxy.ts saw null session → redirected to /login
5. This created an infinite redirect loop: login → dashboard → login

SOLUTION: Use Next-Auth v5 authorized callback pattern:
- Add 'authorized' callback to NextAuth config in lib/auth/index.ts
- This callback receives { auth, request } where request.nextUrl.pathname is the current route
- The callback decides: return true (allow) or false (redirect to login)
- proxy.ts exports: export { auth as proxy } from '@/lib/auth' (or use auth() wrapper)
- Next-Auth handles the cookie reading and redirect automatically

Also fixed: URLs inside (dashboard) group. Group folder (dashboard) does NOT add /dashboard prefix to URLs. So app/(dashboard)/sat/page.tsx has URL /sat, NOT /dashboard/sat. This caused 404 errors on all module links.

Reference: https://authjs.dev/getting-started/session-management/protecting

observations.title: Auth.js v5 Simplified - Removed authorized callback and custom cookies
observations.content:
FINAL FIX for auth redirect loop:

Root causes identified:
1. Using BOTH auth() wrapper in proxy.ts AND authorized callback in NextAuth config caused conflicts. Next-Auth v5 documentation states to use one pattern or the other, never both together.

2. Custom cookie configuration with __Secure-ribotflow-session name and secure: false in development violated browser cookie security policies. The __Secure- prefix requires the Secure attribute to be set to true. Browsers (especially Chrome) reject cookies that violate this rule, meaning the session cookie was never actually stored despite the API endpoint appearing to work.

Solution implemented:
- Removed authorized callback entirely from lib/auth/index.ts
- Removed custom cookies configuration (now using Next-Auth defaults)
- proxy.ts now uses ONLY the auth() wrapper with manual redirect logic
- All page components no longer do redirect('/login') - they just return null if no session (proxy handles all redirects)

This creates a single source of truth for auth: the proxy. The proxy reads the session cookie via auth() wrapper, validates it, and either allows the request or redirects to login. No more double redirects or conflicting auth checks.
